title: Wealthiest Suburbs of St. Louis
content:
Top 20 most expensive suburbs by median sale price (April 2024 - May 2025, 12+ sales minimum).

RANKINGS:
1. Frontenac — $1,610,000
2. Town and Country — $1,493,705
3. Ladue — $1,317,500
4. St. Albans — $960,750
5. Clayton — $847,260
6. Sunset Hills — $760,000
7. Des Peres — $685,000
8. Olivette — $634,000
9. Defiance — $628,950
10. Glendale — $602,000
11. Innsbrook — $586,000
12. Chesterfield — $583,000
13. Wildwood — $572,500
14. Weldon Spring — $567,500
15. Creve Coeur — $537,000
16. Cottleville — $532,874
17. Augusta — $530,000
18. Dardenne Prairie — $512,000
19. Kirkwood — $500,000
20. Webster Groves — $475,000

HIGHLIGHTS:
- Frontenac: Prestigious central corridor, high-end shopping/dining, Ladue School District
- Town and Country: Sprawling estates, peaceful vibe, easy downtown access
- Ladue: Most expensive sale was 1705 N Woodlawn at $13 million
- Clayton: Seat of St. Louis County, biggest economic hub outside the city
- Kirkwood & Webster Groves: Historic charm, walkable downtowns, active communities

NATIONAL CONTEXT: Every Missouri county in STL metro falls below national median home price of $430,848. St. Louis County median was $270,000 — significant affordability advantage.
